name: Stripe webhook signature-failure: add rate-limited Sentry escalation beyond structured log
description: Follow-up from WI-639 review (PR #948, claude[bot] CONSIDER, non-blocking): stripe-webhook.ts signature-verification failure now emits a structured logger.warn event (queryable) but no captureException — deliberate, to avoid alert storms. Gap: sustained misconfiguration produces queryable logs but never pages. Proposal from review: rate-limited/threshold-based captureException or a deduplicated Inngest metric flush when the signature-failure rate exceeds a threshold.
acceptanceCriteria: Outcome: sustained Stripe webhook signature-verification failure (sustained = repeated failures within a bounded window; threshold/window are implementer-chosen and documented) produces a deduplicated escalation signal visible in Sentry, while isolated failures remain log-only (no per-event alert storm — the deliberate WI-639 design holds). Mechanism is implementer's choice within repo conventions (rate-limited captureException, or threshold flush via safeSend()/Inngest); must not block or slow the webhook response path, and must not leak signature/secret material into the signal. Regression: test demonstrating N failures within the window emit exactly one escalation and a single failure emits none. Resend webhook: assess for the same treatment in-scope or record explicit out-of-scope rationale.
